zh.lifeSummary: 當William Thornton於1425年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾易亞斯特 牛頓 白 頓時，他的父親Robert Thornton30歲，母親Isabel Mekylfeld23歲。他於1450年與Agnes Aldborough在英國越可爾牛頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1498年3月17日享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: Thornton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of numerous places throughout England and Scotland so called, from Old English thorn ‘thorn tree’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The placename is most frequent in northern England, especially Yorkshire, where there are at least 16 possible sources for the surname.
Irish: Anglicized (translated) form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin ‘son of Sceachán’ (see Skehan ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Draighneáin ‘descendant of Draighneán’ (see Drennan ).

variants.name: Thornber
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the five minor places in Yorkshire called Thornber, particularly Thornber in Halton West. The placenames derive from Old English thorn ‘thorn’ + beorg ‘hill, mound’.
Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’; compare   Thornton  .

variants.name: Thorton
meanings: English: probably a shortened form of   Thornton  , or a habitational name from Thorton in Marnhull, Dorset, which has the same origin.

variants.name: Skehan
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin or Ó Sceacháin ‘son (or descendant) of Sceachán’, a personal name probably based on a diminutive of sceach ‘briar, thorn bush’. The Gaelic name has sometimes been ‘translated’ as Thornton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Thornton was born in 1425, in East Newton by Hedon, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Thornton, was 30 and his mother, Isabel Mekylfeld, was 23. He married Agnes Aldborough in 1450, in Newton, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 17 March 1498, at the age of 73.

Il a épousé Agnes Aldborough en 1450, à Newton, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 17 mars 1498, à l’âge de 73 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Thornton(이)가 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 뉴턴 비 헤던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Thornton(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Mekylfeld(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1450년 영국 요르크실르 뉴턴에서 Agnes Aldborough(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1498년 3월 17일 73세의 나이로 사망했다.
